methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

data.narrative:
Epirus is a Series D defense and security technology company with $1.35B post-money valuation and $550M+ total raised. The peer set comprises 10 comparable companies across public and private markets: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Saronic, Flock Safety, Chaos Industries, Skydio, and Hidden Level, spanning Series C through Series E.

Two valuation methods triangulate a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a standard 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier to the $1.4B last post-money valuation, implying $2.0B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector comparables—Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Flock Safety ($7.5B Series E), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), Skydio ($2.2B Series E), and Hidden Level ($500M Series C)—produces a median of $4.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B (low) to $5.2B (high), with a median of $3.3B. Epirus's current $1.35B valuation sits below this median, reflecting either a discount relative to comparable stage peers or undervaluation relative to sector precedent. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency metrics and revenue traction relative to the peer set, which exhibit substantial dispersion ($500M–$9.2B) despite similar stages.
